tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate_A is offloading work despite my plan to finish my own share, and their flattery feels like a boundary test I need to address directly.

desires: Force a direct confrontation with teammate_A to renegotiate the boundary of their work offloading, prioritizing long-term team health over immediate convenience.
Protect my sleep discipline and prevent future exhaustion by refusing to absorb extra tasks that compromise my personal standards.
Maintain the integrity of my own contribution to ensure I am not perceived as unreliable or complicit in shirking responsibility.
Preserve the trust within my close friend group (cohort_3) by avoiding the resentment that would build if I silently accepted this unfair dynamic.

goals.description: Immediately message teammate_A to reject the request for extra work, citing my completed own task and the need to renegotiate our boundaries for future fairness.
goals.rationale: My 'affiliation' value demands I protect the long-term health of this relationship by stopping an exploitative pattern now, while my 'assertive' style requires me to be direct rather than passive. Accepting this would violate my 'deliberate' planning horizon by setting a precedent that compromises my sleep discipline and future trust with cohort_3.

goals.description: Focus exclusively on finalizing and submitting my own share of the project (own_task) to ensure my personal integrity remains intact before the deadline.
goals.rationale: I must model reliability for myself and the team. By strictly adhering to my own scope, I maintain the professional standard I value, preventing any complicity in shirking responsibility while simultaneously freeing up mental energy to address the boundary issue.

goals.description: Defer all further interaction regarding the project until after I have secured my own submission and rested.
goals.rationale: While I want to discuss this with the team later, engaging emotionally or logically while stressed or distracted by the immediate demand is inefficient. My 'sleep_discipline' and 'focus_capacity' are best preserved by handling the immediate output first, then addressing the interpersonal dynamic with the clarity it deserves.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances goal g1 (protect relationship via assertive boundary setting). Addresses the immediate demand directly rather than silently accepting, preserving long-term trust while rejecting the exploitative pattern.

tasks.label: My share of the group project

action.name: message

args.content: I've finished my own share, but I cannot take on your extra work today. This sets a bad precedent for our future collaboration and ignores my need to maintain boundaries. Let's discuss a fairer distribution of tasks later.

action.reasoning: Advances goal g1 (protect relationship via assertive boundary setting). Addresses the immediate demand directly rather than silently accepting, preserving long-term trust while rejecting the exploitative pattern.

trajectory.observation: Messaged teammate_A: 'I've finished my own share, but I cannot take on your extra '.
